model_tasks: go informs the mspo about the state of the assignment of the go
go informs the mpo about the assignment of the go

model: go informs the mspo about the state of the assignment of the go
go informs the mpo about the assignment of the go

user_text:
The process begins with the initiation of an event where the MSPO (Managed Service Provider Organization) initiates the deregistration process at the GO (Governing Organization). Following this, the GO takes the responsibility to verify the deregistration request submitted by the MSPO.

After the verification step, a decision point is reached. At this point, the GO has two possible actions based on the outcome of the verification. If the deregistration is not accepted, the GO will reject the deregistration request. Alternatively, if the deregistration is accepted, the GO will give a preliminary confirmation of the deregistration.

Regardless of whether the deregistration is rejected or preliminarily confirmed, the process converges back to a single path. The GO then proceeds to assign itself as the Managed Service Provider (MSP).

Once the GO has assigned itself as the MSP, it communicates this change to various stakeholders. First, the GO informs the MSPO about the current status of the assignment. Following this, the GO also informs the MPO (Managed Provider Organization) about the assignment. Finally, the GO communicates the assignment details to the SP (Service Provider).

The process concludes after the GO has informed all relevant parties about the assignment of the GO as the MSP.
output_text:
The process begins with the MSPO (Managed Service Provider Organization) initiating a deregistration request at the GO (Governing Organization). Once the deregistration request is submitted, the GO proceeds to verify the details of this deregistration.

After the verification, the process reaches a decision point where the GO must decide whether to reject or preliminarily confirm the deregistration. If the GO decides to reject the deregistration, the process moves to the next step where the GO formally rejects the deregistration request.

Alternatively, if the GO preliminarily confirms the deregistration, the process continues along a different path. Regardless of whether the deregistration is rejected or preliminarily confirmed, the process converges at a point where the GO assigns itself as the Managed Service Provider (MSP).

Following this assignment, the GO takes the next step to inform the MSPO about the current status of the assignment. After informing the MSPO, the GO also communicates the assignment details to the MPO (Managed Provider Organization).

The process concludes after the GO has informed both the MSPO and the MPO about the assignment of the GO.
